Career per-36 profile shows solid rebounding (8.34) and blocking (1.86) with modest assists (2.8), consistent with a Center archetype. The elevated 3PM (2.39 per 36) marks him as a stretch-center variant but does not displace him from the Center bucket. All four signals — height, Yahoo position, REB/BLK profile, and role context — converge on Center.

verdicts.name: Jordan Walsh
verdicts.rationale: Walsh is 6'6" (borderline) but Yahoo slots him primary PF, and his career per-36 profile — REB 6.93, AST 1.61, BLK 0.77 — is a classic forward fingerprint: meaningful rebounding, low playmaking, modest shot-blocking. The 1.27 3PM fits a stretch-forward role. Despite metadata labeling him Guard, the Yahoo primary position (PF) and counting-stat profile both align with Forward. His archetype in the 2023-2025 window is a young, versatile wing/forward, not a Guard.

verdicts.name: Pelle Larsson
verdicts.rationale: Larsson is 6'5" with Yahoo primary position SF and eligible at SF/SG. His per-36 profile shows balanced REB (4.21) and AST (3.67) with minimal BLK (0.30) and moderate 3PM (1.31), consistent with a wing/small forward archetype rather than a true guard. Height is borderline (6'5" skews Guard), but Yahoo primary SF and the REB-heavy-relative-to-AST profile tips to Forward. No interior BLK presence rules out Center entirely.

verdicts.name: Antonio Reeves
verdicts.rationale: At 6'5" with a career 3PM per 36 of 3.08, low REB (3.34), low AST (2.27), and negligible BLK (0.24), Reeves is a perimeter shooter with a classic off-ball scoring guard profile. Yahoo lists him SF/SG with SF primary, but his height and stat signature — dominant 3-point volume, minimal playmaking and rebounding — are squarely Guard archetype. Both metadata fields independently confirm Guard. No meaningful Center or power Forward attributes present.

verdicts.name: Trey Jemison III
verdicts.rationale: Jemison fits the Center bucket cleanly on every criterion. At 6'10" with zero 3-point attempts, 11.44 REB/36, 1.63 AST/36, and 1.24 BLK/36, his counting-stat profile is a textbook traditional big: high rebounding, minimal playmaking, no perimeter shooting. Yahoo lists him C primary. His entire career falls within the 2019-26 window (debut 2023), so there is no pre-window role to discount.

verdicts.name: Dalton Knecht
verdicts.rationale: Knecht is 6'6" with Yahoo primary SF, eligible at SF/SG. His per-36 profile (REB 4.96, AST 1.47, BLK 0.35, FG3M 3.04) is a classic scoring wing: moderate rebounding, minimal playmaking, negligible shot-blocking, and high three-point volume. Debuted 2024 so the entire window is current. Height rules out Center; low AST rules out Guard as primary. SF archetype is unambiguous.

verdicts.name: Cody Williams
verdicts.rationale: Cody Williams is a 6'8" (80 inches) wing who debuted in 2024. His Yahoo primary position is SF and he is eligible at SF/SG. His per-36 stat profile — REB 4.08, AST 2.31, BLK 0.58, FG3M 0.82 — fits the Forward archetype: moderate rebounding, modest playmaking, low shot-blocking, and some three-point production. Nothing in the profile suggests Center (no rim-protection, insufficient rebounding dominance) or Guard (AST too low relative to REB). All signals converge on Forward.

verdicts.name: Kyle Filipowski
verdicts.rationale: Filipowski is 6'11" and Yahoo-listed as C (primary) with C/PF eligibility. His 10.63 REB/36 confirms a big man rebounding role. The 3.66 AST/36 and 1.58 FG3M/36 reflect a modern stretch-passing center archetype, not a guard or forward role. BLK/36 of 0.6 is low but does not disqualify Center — many modern skill bigs are not rim-protectors. All signals (height, Yahoo position, metadata, rebounding volume) converge on Center with no meaningful pull toward Forward or Guard.

verdicts.name: Kyshawn George
verdicts.rationale: At 6'8" with a balanced REB/AST profile (6.32/4.27 per 36), meaningful 3-point shooting (2.32 FG3M per 36), and moderate BLK (1.05), George fits the stretch/versatile forward archetype cleanly. AST rate is too high for a Center bucket; REB and BLK are too low for a true big. Yahoo primary PF confirms. No positional ambiguity — Forward is the clear primary bucket.

verdicts.name: Kel'el Ware
verdicts.rationale: Kel'el Ware is a clear Center archetype across all five decision criteria. At 84 inches (7'0"), he is well above the 6'10" Center threshold. His career per-36 profile shows elite rebounding (13.24 REB), solid shot-blocking (1.7 BLK), and very low assists (1.4 AST) — the canonical high-REB/BLK low-AST Center pattern. Yahoo primary position is C. He debuted in 2024 so the entire career window falls within scope. The 1.42 FG3M per 36 suggests some floor-spacing ability but does not dilute the dominant interior archetype given the rebounding and block rates.

verdicts.name: Johnny Furphy
verdicts.rationale: Furphy is 6'8" (80 inches), firmly in the Forward band. Yahoo primary position is SF (eligible SF/SG). Per-36 stats: REB 6.8 (moderate, wing-level), AST 1.46 (too low for Guard), BLK 0.75 (too low for Center). Rebounding wing profile without playmaking fits Forward cleanly. Metadata Guard flag likely reflects SG eligibility, but height and Yahoo primary SF override it. Short career window (2024-25 only) limits certainty, hence medium confidence.

verdicts.name: Jaylon Tyson
verdicts.rationale: Tyson is 6'6" with Yahoo primary position SF and eligible at SF/SG. His per-36 profile shows REB 6.92 (solid wing rebounder, not Guard-level), AST 3.3 (moderate, not a primary playmaker), BLK 0.37 (low, consistent with a wing), and FG3M 1.99 (perimeter shooter). This is a classic scoring wing/small forward archetype. While metadata labels him Guard, the Yahoo primary SF and rebounding rate tip the balance to Forward. The 6'6" height sits on the Guard-Forward boundary but the role is clearly SF-oriented.

verdicts.name: Oso Ighodaro
verdicts.rationale: At 6'11" with Yahoo primary position C, zero three-point shooting, and a rebounding-forward stat profile (8.09 REB, 1.06 BLK per 36), Ighodaro fits the Center bucket clearly. The 3.36 AST/36 reflects modern big-man passing ability but does not override height, position eligibility, and the complete absence of perimeter shooting. All four decision criteria (archetype, height, Yahoo primary, counting-stat profile) converge on Center.

verdicts.name: Jonathan Mogbo
verdicts.rationale: At 6'9" (81 inches) with Yahoo primary position C and C/PF-only eligibility, Mogbo profiles as a modern playmaking center. His REB/36 of 8.83 and BLK/36 of 1.07 anchor him in the big-man bucket, while his AST/36 of 3.53 reflects a passing big rather than a wing forward. Minimal 3-point shooting (0.34/36) rules out stretch-forward archetype. The AST rate is elevated for a center but not enough to shift the bucket — it is consistent with a contemporary PF/C who initiates short-roll offense. Yahoo primary C is the tiebreaker over Forward given the otherwise borderline height.

verdicts.name: Jaylen Wells
verdicts.rationale: Jaylen Wells is a textbook 3-and-D wing. At 6'7" with Yahoo primary position SF, he sits squarely in the Forward bucket. His per-36 profile — 4.67 REB, 2.28 AST, 0.16 BLK, 2.42 3PM — matches a wing forward archetype: moderate rebounding, minimal playmaking, no shot-blocking, active three-point shooting. Nothing in the profile suggests Guard (AST too low, height at ceiling) or Center (BLK near zero, REB moderate). Forward is unambiguous.

verdicts.name: Spencer Jones
verdicts.rationale: Spencer Jones is 6'7", Yahoo primary SF, debuted 2024 so entirely within the window. His per-36 profile (REB 5.06, AST 1.24, BLK 0.70, FG3M 1.07) is a classic wing/stretch-forward signature: moderate rebounding well above Guard range, minimal playmaking, low shot-blocking, and modest 3-point volume. Low AST rules out Guard; low BLK and non-dominant rebounding rule out Center. All four criteria (archetype, height, Yahoo position, counting-stat ratio) converge on Forward.
